trivia_bullets.text: Harpies on Theros are servant creatures of Erebos, god of the dead, sent to torment the living and the recently deceased. Mindwrack Harpy is typed as an Enchantment Creature — Harpy (collector number 276, a showcase-frame variant in the full-art Nyx treatment), reflecting Theros's defining mechanic that divine creatures are woven from the starry fabric of the Nyx [Scryfall].

"Harpies are among the most detested creatures in all of Theros... and they thoroughly deserve it." [Scryfall]. The dry editorial aside is structurally unusual for MTG flavor text — most text inhabits a character's voice; this one addresses the reader directly, acting as lore footnote rather than in-world quote.

Mindwrack Harpy mills three cards from each player at the beginning of your combat — symmetrical milling on a flying enchantment creature. Its EDHREC rank of 6936 is notably high for a common, reflecting adoption in graveyard-matters and Erebos-adjacent Commander decks where the symmetry is exploited for self-mill. Legal in Pauper and PauperCommander [Scryfall, EDHREC].
